confidence_meta.reason: H.4.1 (WALCL/TGA/Reserves) release is dated today (Jul 23) and had not posted at scrape time - within the 48h catalyst window, -8. Additional discretionary -3 for the compounding of fresh Red Sea/Houthi escalation on top of the ongoing Iran/Hormuz conflict plus today's VIX reversal (equity vol no longer calming). MOVE 76.3 still <80 (no formal penalty); scores not cleanly split 2-2 (1 stable, 3 deteriorating).

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Oil Shock Escalating (Red Sea) / Real-Rate Pressure Building / Plumbing Frozen Ahead of H.4.1
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Energy/oil firming on fresh Red Sea escalation; SOXX roughly holding versus SPY; bank-adjacent credit proxies (ALLY/CACC/COF) broadly softer; Korea gate still closed despite today's KRW-asset bounce
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: H.4.1 release later today (Jul 23); FOMC Jul 28-29 (5-6 days), now carrying rate-hike repricing risk per Bloomberg
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: Closed 1/3 - USDKRW pass only; EWY below SMA20 (173.27 vs 180.58); KOSPI below 8,200 (7,096.90)

bias: Plumbing remains frozen ahead of today's H.4.1 print, but the more urgent signal is oil's acceleration - up +6.58% today to $92.54 on fresh Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers and a Red Sea blockade threat, layering onto the already-running Iran/Hormuz conflict (oil now +24% since Jul 8). Bloomberg reports Treasury yields at 2026 highs and markets beginning to price FOMC (Jul 28-29) rate-hike risk rather than cuts. VIX reversed sharply today (+14.5%) after days of calming, now rising in tandem with MOVE (4th straight session higher) - a materially worse vol combination than yesterday's equity-calm/bond-vol-up divergence. Credit proxies (ALLY, CACC, COF) softened again despite still-tight HY OAS, and the real 2Y proxy extended its rise for a 4th straight session.

supporting: Oil (CL1!) +6.58% today, +24% since Jul 8 on Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers and a Red Sea blockade threat, on top of the ongoing Iran/Hormuz conflict
US yield curve pushing to fresh highs (US10Y 4.699%, US30Y 5.170%) - Bloomberg reports 2026 highs and FOMC repricing toward a hike
VIX +14.5% today, breaking a multi-day calming streak, now rising alongside MOVE (4th straight session up)
DFII5/DFII10 (TIPS real yields) continuing to firm for a second session, confirming the market itself is pricing part of the oil shock
USDJPY 163.77, further above the 162 BOJ intervention threshold
against: HY OAS actually tightened slightly (2.69%->2.68%) - no credit-stress confirmation despite the softer single-name proxies

Gold -2.5% vs Jul 22 despite oil's surge - breaks yesterday's 'inflation hedging demand' pattern, suggesting some of today's move is technical/event-driven rather than a durable inflation regime shift
KOSPI +4.40%, Samsung +3.65%, SK Hynix +4.86% today - Asian risk appetite bounced hard, inconsistent with a full flight-to-quality
Reserves and TGA both still frozen at the Jul 15 vintage pending today's H.4.1 - no confirmed plumbing deterioration yet
Oil's move is geopolitical-event-driven (Red Sea blockade threat) rather than demand-driven - could reverse quickly on de-escalation headlines
counterargs.read: Today adds a fresh escalation (Houthi/Red Sea) on top of an already-running Iran/Hormuz oil shock, and for the first time equity vol (VIX) is moving in the same direction as bond vol (MOVE) rather than diverging - a meaningfully worse combination. But credit spreads (HY OAS) still haven't confirmed stress, gold's failure to rally with oil today is a genuine divergence worth watching, and Asian equities bounced hard, none of which fit a clean flight-to-quality. This still reads as an event-driven geopolitical risk premium rather than a structural liquidity or credit event, but the bar for de-escalation just moved higher and the FOMC (Jul 28-29) now carries real two-sided risk.

trade_table.rationale: +6.58% today, +24% since Jul 8 on fresh Houthi/Red Sea escalation atop the Iran/Hormuz conflict; DFII5/DFII10 continuing to firm for a 2nd session confirms the TIPS market is pricing it.

Real 2Y proxy rising for a 4th straight session (1.817%->1.864%->1.915%) as US02Y outpaces flat EXPINF2YR; front-end real-rate pressure persisting into the Jul 28-29 FOMC, now with rate-hike repricing risk.
Gate still closed 1/3; today's bounce (EWY +1.75%, KOSPI +4.40%) doesn't close the gap to SMA20 (180.58) or the 8,200 KOSPI threshold.
SOXX -0.82% today but SOXX/SPY ratio roughly flat at 0.746 - relative resilience versus SPY (-1.14%) and QQQ (-1.72%) even as the tape sells off on yields.
All three down again today despite HY OAS actually tightening slightly - the divergence between index-level credit and single-name proxies is now a 4th-session pattern for CACC.

catalysts.note: Dated for release today; had not posted at scrape time (values still Jul 15 vintage). First plumbing move in over a week.
Press conference 2:30pm ET Jul 29. No SEP at this meeting. Bloomberg reports markets now pricing rate-hike risk given the oil-driven yield surge - a reversal from prior cut expectations.

catalysts.event: Iran/Hormuz conflict + fresh Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers, Red Sea blockade threat
catalysts.note: Primary driver of oil's rise, now +24% since Jul 8; DFII5/DFII10 and, as of today, T5YIE/T10YIE all reacting.
Sources conflicted on exact date (Jul 23 vs Jul 29) as of Jul 22; SK Hynix +4.86% today, unclear if earnings-related. Confirm on next run.
Key inflation print given oil's rise; first full read since the Red Sea escalation.

plumbing.tga_delta: flat vs Jul 22 - still Jul 15 H.4.1 vintage, release dated today
plumbing.reserves_delta: flat vs Jul 22 - same H.4.1 vintage

duration_score.classification: Real-Rate Repricing Continuing / Oil Shock Escalating (Red Sea)
duration_score.reason: US02Y rose again (+5.1bps to 4.355%) while EXPINF2YR stayed flat at 2.44% for a 4th session, pushing the real 2Y proxy to 1.915% (from 1.864% Jul22) - continuing the front-end real-rate-tightening signature for a 4th straight session. DFII5 (+4bps to 2.09%) and DFII10 (+2bps to 2.37%) both firmed again. Oil's fresh acceleration (+6.58% today on Houthi/Red Sea escalation, +24% since Jul 8) combined with today's VIX reversal raises the risk this tips into genuine inflation reacceleration rather than pure term-premium drift.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Front-end staying sticky/rising into today's H.4.1 print and the Jul 28-29 FOMC, while a fresh Red Sea escalation adds a second, independent inflationary force on top of the running Iran/Hormuz conflict.
james_framework.pain_trade: Real-rate pressure via a rising nominal front end against a still-flat 2Y breakeven proxy - not yet full CPI panic, but breakevens (T5YIE/T10YIE) ticked up today for the first time, a signal worth watching closely.
james_framework.watch_signal: If nominal 2Y starts falling even as oil stays elevated, that signals a growth-scare/cuts narrative overriding the inflation risk; if T5YIE/T10YIE continue rising in tandem with oil, that confirms a shift toward genuine inflation reacceleration.

t2yie.label: 2Y breakeven inflation
t2yie.note: T2YIE is not a real FRED metric - retired from this monitor. EXPINF2YR used as the 2Y proxy instead.

t5yie.label: 5Y breakeven
t5yie.note: +2bps vs Jul 22 (2.28->2.30), first move after several flat sessions.

t10yie.label: 10Y breakeven
t10yie.note: +2bps vs Jul 22, tracking oil's continued rise.

expinf2yr.label: 2Y expected inflation
expinf2yr.note: Unchanged for a 4th straight session despite nominal 2Y rising.

dfii5.label: 5Y TIPS real yield (primary)
dfii5.note: +4bps vs Jul 22 (2.05->2.09) - TIPS market continuing to firm for a 2nd session.

real_yield_2y.note: Up from 1.864% Jul 22 - continuing the real-rate-tightening signature.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: Breakevens (T5YIE/T10YIE) both ticked up today for the first time in several sessions, alongside DFII5/DFII10 firming again - the clearest sign yet that the TIPS and inflation-expectations markets are starting to price the Red Sea escalation, not just the term structure.

us10y.note: +4.9bps vs Jul 22, +12.2bps vs Jul 8 - Bloomberg reports 2026 highs on oil-driven Fed repricing.

us30y.note: +2.9bps vs Jul 22 to 5.170%, fresh high in this series.

us02y.note: +5.1bps vs Jul 22 to 4.355% - rate-hike risk repricing lifting the front end.

spread_2s10s.note: 0.344% vs 0.346% Jul 22 - front end and long end rising in step today.

fx.usdjpy_note: Further above the 162 BOJ threshold vs Jul 22 (163.108->163.771), +0.41%

sector_rotation.current_regime: Oil Shock Escalating (Red Sea) / Real-Rate Pressure Building / Plumbing Frozen Ahead of H.4.1

actionable_output.rotation_into: Oil/energy on the fresh Red Sea escalation; SOXX holding up relatively well versus SPY/QQQ
actionable_output.rotation_out_of: Long duration Treasuries (TLT), bank-adjacent credit proxies (ALLY, CACC, COF), and high-multiple growth (QQQ)
actionable_output.hedge: RRP buffer still minimal; today's H.4.1 print is the key structural catalyst; watch T5YIE/T10YIE (both ticked up today) for confirmation of a genuine inflation-reacceleration regime versus a fading geopolitical premium

sector_rotation.note: The move from 'inflation_reacceleration/liquidity_expansion' (Jul22, Low-Medium confidence) to a clearer inflation_reacceleration read today is driven by the fresh Red Sea escalation plus the first VIX/MOVE co-movement in over a week - but gold's failure to rally with oil and HY OAS actually tightening argue the market hasn't fully committed to the regime shift yet.

data_notes.run_continuity: This run continues the daily series through Jul 22, 2026 (prior run). All deltas above are vs Jul 22 close unless noted.
data_notes.watchlist_gap: The 'enabling-layer thesis chain' tickers (CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI, EOSE) remain absent from the 'Liquidity Monitor - Jae' TradingView watchlist (36 symbols). DRAM is the only chain ticker still tracked.
data_notes.h41_timing: H.4.1 (WALCL/WTREGEN/WRESBAL) is dated for release today (Jul 23) but had not posted at scrape time (19:27 CEST / 17:27 UTC); values reflect the Jul 15 vintage. Re-pull later today or on the next run to capture the fresh print.
data_notes.catalyst_source: FOMC (Jul 28-29), CPI (Aug 12), NFP (Aug 7) dates and the Red Sea/Houthi oil-shock narrative confirmed via live web search this run (Bloomberg, NBC News, Washington Post), not a connected economic calendar feed.
